content.text: When streaming with the Claude API, you receive
that you must handle appropriately. Use the
for simple text-only streaming (it automatically extracts
events), or iterate over raw events with
(TypeScript) to get the accumulated complete message after the stream finishes. Handle errors by checking

┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ STREAMING EVENT LIFECYCLE: ORDER OF EVENTS         │
├───────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                     │
│ 1. message_start (ONE TIME)                        │
│    - Stream begins                                 │
│    - Contains empty Message object                 │
│    - Has: id, model, role, initial usage           │
│                                                     │
│ 2. content_block_start (PER BLOCK)                │
│    - New content block begins (text/tool/thinking) │
│    - Has: index, block type                        │
│                                                     │
│ 3. content_block_delta (MULTIPLE TIMES)           │
│    - Main body—incremental updates                 │
│    - Has: index, delta type, delta content         │
│    - text_delta: "text": "..."                     │
│    - thinking_delta: "thinking": "..."             │
│    - input_json_delta: "partial_json": "..."       │
│                                                     │
│ 4. content_block_stop (PER BLOCK)                 │
│    - Content block ends                            │
│    - Has: index                                    │
│                                                     │
│ 5. message_delta (ONE TIME)                        │
│    - Final metadata before completion              │
│    - Has: stop_reason, final usage                 │
│                                                     │
│ 6. message_stop (ONE TIME)                         │
│    - Stream complete                               │
│    - Empty data: {}                                │
│                                                     │
│ Optional: ping events (between any events)         │
│ Optional: error events (if overloaded/failure)     │
│                                                     │
└───────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

One message = 1 start + N deltas + 1 stop per block
Two Approaches: SDK Helpers vs Raw Events
Approach 1: SDK Text Helpers (Recommended for Simple Cases)
Chat interfaces, CLIs, simple text streaming

import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

stream = client.messages.create(
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a poem about AI."}],
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    stream=True,
)

# ✅ Simple: Just print text as it's generated
for text in stream.text_stream:
    print(text, end="", flush=True)

print()  # New line after stream completes

# Get final accumulated message
final_message = stream.get_final_message()
print(final_message.to_json())

from each event
- Concatenates into strings
- You get clean text, not raw SSE JSON
Approach 2: Raw Event Iteration (For Advanced Handling)
Tool use, thinking blocks, custom event handling, progress tracking

import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

stream = client.messages.create(
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a poem about AI."}],
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    stream=True,
)

# ✅ Advanced: Handle each event type manually
for event in stream:
    # Check event type
    event_type = event.type
    
    if event_type == "message_start":
        print(f"Message started: {event.message.id}")
        print(f"Model: {event.message.model}")
        
    elif event_type == "content_block_start":
        print(f"Content block {event.index} started: {event.content_block.type}")
        
    elif event_type == "content_block_delta":
        # Extract delta content
        delta = event.delta
        
        if delta.type == "text_delta":
            # Text streaming
            print(delta.text, end="", flush=True)
            
        elif delta.type == "thinking_delta":
            # Extended thinking (Sonnet 4.5+)
            print(f"[Thinking]: {delta.thinking}", end="", flush=True)
            
        elif delta.type == "input_json_delta":
            # Tool use—partial JSON
            print(f"[Tool input]: {delta.partial_json}", end="", flush=True)
        
    elif event_type == "content_block_stop":
        print(f"\nContent block {event.index} finished")
        
    elif event_type == "message_delta":
        print(f"\nStop reason: {event.delta.stop_reason}")
        print(f"Output tokens: {event.usage.output_tokens}")
        
    elif event_type == "message_stop":
        print("Stream complete!")
        
    elif event_type == "error":
        print(f"Error: {event.error}")
        break

print()  # New line

# Get accumulated final message
final_message = stream.get_final_message()
print(f"\nFull response: {final_message.content[0].text}")

import asyncio
import anthropic

client = anthropic.AsyncAnthropic()

async def main():
    stream = await client.messages.create(
        max_tokens=1024,
        messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Write a poem about AI."}],
        model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
        stream=True,
    )
    
    # Async text stream (simple)
    async for text in stream.text_stream:
        print(text, end="", flush=True)
    
    print()
    
    # or async raw events (advanced)
    async for event in stream:
        if event.type == "content_block_delta":
            if event.delta.type == "text_delta":
                print(event.delta.text, end="", flush=True)
    
    message = await stream.get_final_message()
    print(f"\nFull: {message.content[0].text}")

asyncio.run(main())
Example 1: Chat Interface with Progress Indicator

import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

messages = [{"role": "user", "content": "Explain quantum computing"}]

stream = client.messages.create(
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=messages,
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    stream=True,
)

print("Claude: ", end="", flush=True)

full_text = ""
for text in stream.text_stream:
    full_text += text
    print(text, end="", flush=True)

print()

# Add to conversation history
messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": full_text})

import anthropic

client = anthropic.Anthropic()

tools = [{"name": "get_weather", "description": "Get weather", ...}]

stream = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-opus-4-8",
    max_tokens=1024,
    tools=tools,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Weather in SF?"}],
    stream=True,
)

tool_input = ""

for event in stream:
    if event.type == "content_block_delta":
        if event.delta.type == "text_delta":
            print(event.delta.text, end="", flush=True)
            
        elif event.delta.type == "input_json_delta":
            # Accumulate partial JSON
            tool_input += event.delta.partial_json
            print(f"\n[Tool: {event.delta.name} input: {tool_input}]", flush=True)
    
    elif event.type == "content_block_stop":
        print()  # New line after tool input complete

final_message = stream.get_final_message()

stream = client.messages.create(
    max_tokens=1024,
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}],
    model="claude-sonnet-4-6",
    stream=True,
)

for text in stream.text_stream:
    print(text, end="", flush=True)

print()

# Wait for stream to complete and get full Message
final_message = stream.get_final_message()

# Access full response
print(final_message.content[0].text)  # Complete text
print(final_message.usage.input_tokens)  # Input tokens
print(final_message.usage.output_tokens)  # Output tokens
print(final_message.stop_reason)  # "end_turn", "max_tokens", etc.
. If you break early, it may not return complete data.
If you're not using an SDK, you must parse SSE manually:
— tool use, thinking, custom progress tracking
— this is the main text event
The SDKs make streaming easy—just use
